hook.text: Dozens of times in history, groups sold everything and waited for the world to end. It never did.

buildup.text: Millenarian movements believe in imminent divine transformation — a new world replacing the corrupt old.

discovery.text: These movements often arise among the dispossessed. When earthly hope dies, cosmic hope takes over.

twist.text: When prophecy fails, believers rarely quit. They reinterpret, recalculate, and often grow more committed.

climax.text: Millenarian belief reveals how religion processes despair and channels it into collective expectation.

quiz.question: What typically happens when a millenarian prophecy fails?
options: Believers reinterpret and often grow stronger
